title: Choose a versioning scheme for the surface
description: A decision procedure for selecting URI, header, or media-type versioning based on client needs and how the surface will evolve.
body: Use when launching an API or formalizing how versions will be expressed. Do not pick a scheme by habit without weighing consumer ergonomics. Steps: assess whether clients are browsers, SDKs, or services; compare URI major versioning for visibility, header versioning for cleaner URLs, and media-type versioning for fine-grained content negotiation; decide what constitutes a breaking change that warrants a bump; document the policy. Validation signals include consumers selecting a version predictably and additive changes not requiring a bump. Risk boundary: switching schemes after launch is itself breaking, so choose deliberately and record the rule for what triggers a new version.
